title: coronilla repanda subsp. dura
intro: Coronilla repanda subsp. dura, also known as Dura Bird's Foot, is a flowering plant species in the pea family (Fabaceae) and is a subspecies of Coronilla repanda. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and expectorant effects. The health benefits of Coronilla repanda subsp. dura include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce pain and inflammation. Coronilla repanda subsp. dura is commonly prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, often used to treat various health conditions.
